en.extract:
The yellow-browed camaroptera (Camaroptera superciliaris) is a species of bird in the family Cisticolidae.
It is found in Angola, Benin, Cameroon, Central African Republic, Republic of the Congo, Democratic Republic of the Congo, Ivory Coast, Equatorial Guinea, Gabon, Ghana, Guinea, Liberia, Nigeria, Sierra Leone, Togo, and Uganda.
Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests and subtropical or tropical moist shrubland.
The yellow-browed camaroptera was described by the English zoologist Louis Fraser in 1843 under the binomial name Sylvicola superciliaris. The type locality is the island of Bioko (formerly Fernando Pó) in the Gulf of Guinea off the west coast of Africa. The specific epithet superciliaris is from the Neo-Latin superciliaris,  "eyebrowed" from the classical Latin supercilium, "eyebrow".

The '''yellow-browed camaroptera''' ('''''Camaroptera superciliaris''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Cisticolidae]].
It is found in [[Angola]], [[Benin]], [[Cameroon]], [[Central African Republic]], [[Republic of the Congo]], [[Democratic Republic of the Congo]], [[Ivory Coast]], [[Equatorial Guinea]], [[Gabon]], [[Ghana]], [[Guinea]], [[Liberia]], [[Nigeria]], [[Sierra Leone]], [[Togo]], and [[Uganda]].
Its natural [[habitat]]s are subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s and subtropical or tropical moist [[shrubland]].

The yellow-browed camaroptera was [[Species description|described]] by the English zoologist [[Louis Fraser]] in 1843 under the [[binomial name]] ''Sylvicola superciliaris''. The [[type locality (biology)|type locality]] is the island of [[Bioko]] (formerly Fernando Pó) in the [[Gulf of Guinea]] off the west coast of Africa.<ref>{{cite journal | last=Fraser | first=Louis | author-link=Louis Fraser | year=1843 |  title=''Sylvicola superciliaris'' | journal=Annals and Magazine of Natural History; Zoology, Botany, and Geology | volume=12 | page=440 | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/22101345 }}</ref><ref>{{cite book | editor1-last=Mayr | editor1-first=Ernst | editor1-link=Ernst Mayr | editor2-last=Cottrell | editor2-first=G. William | year=1986 | title=Check-list of Birds of the World | volume=11 | publisher=Museum of Comparative Zoology | place=Cambridge, Massachusetts | page=190 | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/14483891 }}</ref> The [[Specific name (zoology)|specific epithet]] ''superciliaris'' is from the [[Neo-Latin]] ''superciliaris'',  "eyebrowed" from the classical Latin ''supercilium'', "eyebrow".<ref>{{cite book | last=Jobling | first=James A. | year=2010| title=The Helm Dictionary of Scientific Bird Names | publisher=Christopher Helm | location=London | isbn= 978-1-4081-2501-4 | page=374 | url=https://archive.org/stream/Helm_Dictionary_of_Scientific_Bird_Names_by_James_A._Jobling#page/n374/mode/1up }}</ref>
